title: Automated Summarization of Clinical Notes: A Comparative Evaluation
abstract: We evaluate three approaches to automatic summarization of clinical progress notes, comparing extractive, abstractive, and hybrid methods on a manually annotated corpus of ICU notes. Results suggest that abstractive methods outperform extractive baselines on ROUGE but underperform on clinician preference ratings.

title: Phenotyping Acute Kidney Injury in EHR Data: A Systematic Comparison of Rule-Based and ML Approaches
venue: Journal of the American Medical Informatics Association
abstract: This study compares rule-based and machine learning phenotyping algorithms for acute kidney injury using administrative and clinical data from a large academic medical center. We evaluate algorithm performance across patient subgroups and identify sources of systematic disagreement.

title: Alert Fatigue in Clinical Decision Support: A Scoping Review
abstract: We conducted a scoping review of alert fatigue in CDS systems, synthesizing evidence on override rates, contextual factors, and mitigation strategies. We identify gaps in prospective evaluation and propose a framework for studying fatigue in deployment contexts.

title: Poster: Threshold Selection Under Asymmetric Costs in Sepsis Prediction
abstract: We examine how standard threshold selection methods perform when false negatives carry substantially higher clinical cost than false positives, using a sepsis prediction dataset. Results motivate cost-sensitive threshold selection as a default for clinical ML models.
